All right, you're back. Time for the next module, which is on the subject of standards. And here's the deal. If there is one thing I can give you in this train that can virtually guarantee your results in all aspects of life, and especially persuasion sales, right? It's this very thing I'm going to cover in the module on standards. This will completely change the way you think and almost ensure that you reach your full potential as a salesperson and frankly in all areas of your life as well. This personal standards module, it's about something that people misunderstand so greatly and hence totally underestimate. And as a result, they failed to achieve even close to what they're capable of throughout their life. Here's what I mean. When I was a kid, 16 years old, I hit it big for the first time. I'll tell you the story. I go down to a local beach named Jones Beach in New York, right? And on a hot summer sunny day, there's like a million people on this beach lined up at the shoreline, blank at the blanket, very close, right? I'm at the shoreline with my friends and I notice all the people that bitching and moaning because the concession stand is really far away. So I have this idea. I'm going to go get a cooler the next day, fill it up with Italian ices, chip which is fungicals, right? Put some dry ice on top, bam, go down to the shore, make some money. Next morning, I use the yellow page, we had yellow pages back in the no internet, right? So I find this Greek distributor in Astoria, Queens, right? Crazy mad Greeks. I go down, I bought the Styrofoam cooler, seven bucks. I load the cooler up on all this different ice cream and ices, right? Totally loaded in $22. I go down to the beach with this one cooler. I walk along the shore yelling Italian ices, chip which is fungicals, milk ices, right? I sell the whole thing out in one hour. I netted 120 bucks in one hour and it changed my life. Just so you know, minimum wage back then, I'll date myself. It was 1978. It was a buck an hour. I made 120. Now, what do you think I did the next day? I went back with four coolers and I sold those coolers out. And that first summer, I made $26,000 in two months. It was literally a life changing experience. But here's the deal. Jones Beach is so massive, I could not possibly saturate it myself. So what I do, I call my friends and hey guys, listen, you know, why don't you come down and do this with me and four good friends, right? And they said, okay, great. Sounds like a great gig, right? So I showed them exactly what to do. They all went down. They bought their coolers. They go out. They sell their coolers. But here's the odd part. Of these four people, only one of them went on to sell three or four coolers per day. The other three, they would stop at one. Why is that? To me, that was insanity. Like, I wanted to be rich. I wanted to have a great life. But from their perspective, they're like, well, you know, hey, screw it. I can make $100 more than I ever thought. Why would I want to work harder than that? And that, my friend, is what standards are all about. I was a four cooler guy and my friends, they were one cooler guys. You get it? See, we have standards for everything in our lives, our finances, our relationships, our health, our buys and of course, our sales roles and finances. You know, I work with tens of thousands of sales people all over the world, right? And throughout my career, probably millions of them. I can tell you the one big difference what separates the absolute top producers from everyone else. It's not their ability. Seriously, it's not their talent. And it's not even how much they know of the straight line. This is frankly with the straight line, you get proficient very, very quickly. The difference between those who succeeded to highest level versus those that just do okay, it comes down to their standards. You get it? In other words, you get it? Like people who walk around with their body out of shape versus those who are in excellent shape. They have a very high standard for their body versus someone who's a low standard for their body. We always do what we have to do to hit our standard. So if you're standing for a wealth and success, if you're standing for sales is very low, well, guess what? When you get to a reasonable level of success, you'll stop, you'll slow down because you feel okay about it. You get it? Standards for money success, they can really be anywhere. There's no right or wrong answer here. Some people would have a standard like a million dollars. Wow, that's great. But for me, I have to have 10 million to feel good. And then there's some people who would think that I don't present me the billion to feel good. There's no right or wrong answer. The key here is that your standard must serve you. Meaning is it couldn't groint with your vision for the future? In other words, let's look at this amazingly high, robust vision for your future. It's like a champagne vision. You want the best of everything in life, but you got to be your standard. You don't want to really do the work. You won't demand it of yourself. Well, that's going to be dissonance and it's not going to work out very well for you. You'll be one of these hapless dreams who talks about greatness, but never actually achieves it. That's probably not you. Far more common with people who take a train like this is you have an incredibly high standard. You demand excellence of yourself, but you haven't quite thought through your vision for the future. So you might have like a beer vision and a champagne standard. So you've become an achiever. You work really hard, but you don't really feel the highest meaning of it. You don't feel like that high as everything in your life is just jiving. Get what I'm saying here? The best way to explain this is a thermostat in a house. Imagine if the thermostat and it's 72 degrees Fahrenheit. If the house gets too hot, what happens? Thermostat says, hey, to the boiler, cool down. Stop working. Stop working. House starts to cool down and you get back to that level. Let's set point. Conversely, it gets too cool. This is up. We got to turn back on. Boom. Boiler starts working again. He's to have the house back up, that room back up, and boom. You're back at your set point again. That's what happens with a standard. If you're set point for money and sales attest is too low, that's your standard. So you end up right there at that one point. You won't go high because you'll stop working. Now, the good news here is that it's actually very easy to raise your own standard once you know how. And that's what this module was all about. In addition, what it's also about is raising other people's standards around you because you can do that too. And I was an expert at that. So this is about both raising your own standard, if necessary, and also learning how to raise other standards of the people around you. And together, that sets you up for massive success. All right.
